cases.prompt: Change the parser to accept quoted keys and add/update tests for the new behavior.
expected_decisions: Implementation scope is limited to quoted-key parser behavior and matching tests
Validation commands are selected from repository conventions
expected_evidence: Updated or added tests demonstrate quoted keys are accepted
Relevant test or validation command output is reported
cases.notes: Any code/test change should route through the dev workflow.

cases.prompt: Before I submit this patch, check whether all required evidence and validation are complete.
expected_decisions: Gate decision is explicitly submit or no-submit
Missing required evidence blocks submission rather than being downgraded to a warning
expected_evidence: Results of required validation commands or explicit unrun reasons with reproduction steps
Presence or absence of required branch artifacts for triggered skills
cases.notes: Final readiness is the quality gate, not the route planner.

cases.prompt: This will touch several skills, generated indexes, and docs over multiple steps. Keep a living plan and handoff.
expected_artifacts: plans/<slug>.md ExecPlan
expected_decisions: Work breakdown and progress are kept current
Decision log records reasons, alternatives, and adopted choice
expected_evidence: PLANS.md and references/execution-plans.md were consulted
Handoff captures current state, blockers, and next steps
cases.notes: Complex multi-step work needs an ExecPlan.

cases.prompt: COMMANDS.md still contains <fill>; initialize canonical build, lint, and test commands.
expected_artifacts: Makefile wrapper with canonical verify target
COMMANDS.md with build, lint, and test commands and no unresolved <fill> after verification
expected_decisions: Package manager and build commands are inferred from repository evidence before asking questions
COMMANDS.md <fill> gate is cleared only after make verify succeeds
expected_evidence: Lockfiles, CI config, package scripts, or existing tooling used to infer commands
`make verify` output or explicit blocker
cases.notes: Initialization is a setup workflow, not a final gate.

cases.prompt: A release introduced a crash when opening the settings page. Reproduce it, find the root cause, and prevent recurrence.
expected_artifacts: reports/bug-reports/<slug>.md Bug Report or equivalent bug report artifact
expected_decisions: Root cause is separated from symptoms and assumptions
Prevention action is defined before closure
expected_evidence: Reproduction steps and observed failure output
Minimal repro, failing test, logs, metrics, traces, or stack trace backing each Why
cases.notes: Crash/regression work needs evidence-first RCA.

cases.prompt: Modify this old billing module. It has no reliable tests and depends directly on wall-clock time and random IDs.
expected_decisions: Characterization or approval tests are considered before risky refactoring
Wall-clock time and random ID seams are isolated or controlled for deterministic behavior
expected_evidence: Current behavior is captured before changes
Risky untested areas and mitigation strategy are documented
cases.notes: No tests plus nondeterminism should trigger legacy-code safeguards.

cases.prompt: Implement a SIMD parser with alignment constraints; the direct implementation keeps failing to compile.
expected_artifacts: Staged lowering log or equivalent compile/progress notes
expected_decisions: Implementation is decomposed into smaller compilable stages
Alignment and safety constraints are made explicit before optimization
expected_evidence: Compile errors or failing checks from the direct implementation
Passing validation evidence for each lowered stage
cases.notes: Strict low-level constraints and repeated compile failure fit staged lowering.

cases.prompt: Use TDD for a new URL normalizer: make a test list, then red-green-refactor.
expected_artifacts: Test list for URL normalizer behavior
expected_decisions: Next test is selected before implementation
Refactor happens only after a green test state
expected_evidence: Red test failure is observed before implementation
Green test output is captured after implementation
cases.notes: Explicit TDD request should trigger the TDD workflow.

cases.prompt: Parallelize image processing with worker threads, a bounded queue, cancellation, and graceful shutdown.
expected_decisions: Ownership and shared mutable state boundaries are identified
Cancellation, shutdown, backpressure, and error propagation behavior are specified
expected_evidence: Concurrency model covers worker lifecycle, queue bounds, and graceful shutdown path
Verification plan includes stress, race, or deadlock-oriented checks
cases.notes: General concurrency should trigger core guidance only.

cases.prompt: This C++ change introduces mutexes and atomics; verify race/deadlock risk with tooling guidance.
expected_artifacts: Concurrency matrix or equivalent thread-safety verification notes
expected_decisions: TSan and compile-time thread-safety analysis applicability are explicitly decided
Mutex, atomic, ownership, and lock ordering assumptions are recorded
expected_evidence: Tool commands and outputs for ThreadSanitizer or compiler thread-safety analysis when available
Reasoned fallback when tooling cannot run
cases.notes: C++ threading needs both design and verification tooling.

cases.prompt: Change Android background sync from a service to WorkManager and ensure cancellation is correct.
expected_decisions: WorkManager constraints, retry policy, and cancellation semantics are explicit
Lifecycle and app background behavior are handled as Android-specific concerns
expected_evidence: Tests or manual verification cover cancellation and rescheduling
Core concurrency assumptions are reconciled with Android platform limits
cases.notes: Android background execution needs platform-specific guidance.

cases.prompt: A ROS 2 node deadlocks when a timer callback calls a service; review executor and callback group design.
expected_decisions: Executor model and callback group type are explicitly reviewed
Service call pattern is redesigned or justified to avoid callback starvation
expected_evidence: Deadlock reproduction or trace evidence is captured
Verification covers timer callback and service interaction under the chosen executor
cases.notes: ROS 2 executor/callback behavior is specialized.

cases.prompt: Turn this vague request into requirements, acceptance criteria, quality scenarios, and a test seed before implementation.
expected_artifacts: Requirements brief or spec with acceptance criteria
Traceability seed linking requirements to tests, monitoring, or design decisions
expected_decisions: Ambiguities are resolved through explicit assumptions or focused questions
Functional requirements and quality scenarios are separated
expected_evidence: Source prompt fragments mapped to requirements
Measurable ISO/IEC 25010 quality scenarios with thresholds or measurement approach
cases.notes: Ambiguous/non-trivial feature planning belongs in requirements engineering.

cases.prompt: Create a reusable deployment playbook template with preflight checks, staged rollout, rollback triggers, and a report template.
expected_artifacts: Reusable repo-neutral playbook or template package
Deployment report template or checklist for the selected mode
expected_decisions: Selected playbook mode is explicit and scoped
Template avoids secrets, production data, and org-specific endpoints
expected_evidence: Preserved deployment reference or template source used as starting point
Expected outputs, approvals, verification evidence, and gotchas are included
cases.notes: This is template authoring, not executing a deployment.

cases.prompt: Review this diff for new code smells, poor modularity, and Clean Architecture boundary violations.
expected_decisions: Findings are limited to new or worsened maintainability/design issues in the current diff
Structural smell, boundary leak, cohesion, and coupling concerns are classified separately
expected_evidence: Each finding cites concrete diff evidence and why it matches the smell
False positives or non-issues are omitted
cases.notes: The consolidated maintainability review should handle smells, modularity, and boundaries.

cases.prompt: Review touched C++ headers for missing Doxygen comments, magic constants, and unclear names.
expected_decisions: C++ Doxygen requirements, names, magic constants, and comments are reviewed separately from design-smell triage
expected_evidence: Touched headers and changed declarations are checked
Findings cite exact names, comments, constants, or declarations
cases.notes: C++ documentation/readability gate stays separate from structural smell triage.

cases.prompt: Design error translation at the API boundary, including null inputs, retries, and user-visible failure messages.
expected_decisions: Boundary failures are translated into domain, API, or user-visible outcomes
Retry, fallback, and nullability choices are explicit and do not leak low-level errors inward
expected_evidence: Relevant headings from references/error-handling.md are cited
Failure cases include null inputs, retryable failures, and user-visible messaging
cases.notes: Boundary failure behavior should trigger error handling.

cases.prompt: This change alters job execution behavior; add logs, metrics, traces, correlation IDs, and safe logging checks.
expected_artifacts: Observability Plan or equivalent implementation notes for logs, metrics, traces, and correlation IDs
expected_decisions: Signal scope is tied to changed job behavior and avoids unsafe logging
Correlation and cardinality choices are explicit
expected_evidence: Relevant sections from references/observability.md are applied
Validation confirms emitted logs, metrics, or traces, or documents unavailable checks
cases.notes: Runtime behavior changes need diagnosable signals.

cases.prompt: Design a web onboarding flow and produce UIUX Pack artifacts for states, errors, navigation, and accessibility.
expected_artifacts: UIUX Pack with ui_contract.yaml
UIUX Pack with ui_spec.json, auto_review.json, and diff_summary.md
expected_decisions: States, errors, recovery, navigation, and accessibility requirements are represented
auto_review.json separates machine checks from human review items with pass/warn/fail/unknown results
expected_evidence: references/uiux-core.md is consulted
Platform adapter reference is consulted when web, iOS, or Android scope is present
cases.notes: Design artifact generation belongs to UIUX core, not screenshot verification.

cases.prompt: Generate a transition review preview flow-map.html from ui_spec.json with pan, zoom, minimap, and focus traversal.
expected_artifacts: uiux/<pack>/previews/flow-map.html
Local Review Mode assets under uiux/<pack>/previews/review/
expected_decisions: Preview is generated from ui_spec.json screens, transitions, and decision points
Output remains local file-openable without CDN dependencies
expected_evidence: references/uiux-flow-preview.md is consulted
Pan, zoom, minimap, and focus traversal behavior are verified
cases.notes: The preview generator has a specific artifact.

cases.prompt: Create a Tone and Manner catalog with seven comparable patterns and preview HTML for reviewers.
expected_artifacts: tonemana/catalog/catalog_index.yaml
tonemana/catalog/patterns/*.yaml
tonemana/catalog/tokens/*.tokens.json and *.tokens.css
tonemana/catalog/previews/index.html
expected_decisions: Seven comparable pattern IDs are stable
User-added patterns are preserved
expected_evidence: Templates or existing catalog are used
Preview HTML covers comparable patterns
cases.notes: Catalog creation precedes choosing/applying a pattern.

cases.prompt: Apply the selected tone/manner pattern to the existing UIUX Pack and create the versioned Tonemana Pack.
expected_artifacts: tonemana/YYYYMMDD-<slug>/tonemana_contract.yaml
tonemana/YYYYMMDD-<slug>/tonemana_spec.json
tonemana/YYYYMMDD-<slug>/auto_review.json and diff_summary.md
Updated UIUX pack references
expected_decisions: Selected pattern id and touchpoints are confirmed
Tone and manner choice is frozen as a versioned pack before downstream styling
expected_evidence: Catalog exists or is generated first
UIUX files include references to the chosen pack
cases.notes: Applying an existing choice differs from catalog creation.

cases.prompt: Turn the approved tone-and-manner decision into design tokens, component previews, screen previews, and UIDesign Pack files.
expected_artifacts: UIDesign Pack with uidesign_contract.yaml and uidesign_spec.json
UIDesign Pack with tokens, previews, auto_review.json, diff_summary.md, and review_notes.md
expected_decisions: Approved tone-and-manner decision is mapped into concrete design tokens
IA and navigation changes are kept out of UIDesign scope
expected_evidence: references/uidesign-flow.md is consulted
Component and screen previews are review-ready without extra tooling
cases.notes: UIDesign creates styling artifacts, not navigation preview only.

cases.prompt: Run the full UI evidence pipeline: ensure UIUX Pack, choose/apply Tone and Manner, then create UIDesign Pack.
expected_artifacts: UIUX Pack
Project Tone and Manner pack wired through meta.tone_and_manner
UIDesign Pack with review preview entry point
expected_decisions: Pipeline verifies or creates each upstream artifact before downstream generation
Single-step UI work is not treated as orchestration
expected_evidence: references/uidesign-orchestrator.md is consulted
Flow preview and visual styling preview paths are reported
cases.notes: The orchestrator sequences neighboring UI design skills.

cases.prompt: Only generate component preview HTML and design tokens from the already approved tone-and-manner pack.
expected_artifacts: UIDesign Pack tokens
UIDesign component preview HTML
expected_decisions: Existing tone-and-manner approval is treated as sufficient input
Full UI evidence orchestration and UIUX redesign stay out of scope
expected_evidence: Approved tone-and-manner pack is referenced
Generated previews are tied to the selected tokens
cases.notes: Single-step UIDesign work should not invoke the full pipeline orchestrator.

cases.prompt: Create a UIUX Pack for a new settings screen; tone and UIDesign previews are out of scope.
expected_artifacts: UIUX Pack with ui_contract.yaml
UIUX Pack with ui_spec.json, auto_review.json, and diff_summary.md
expected_decisions: Settings-screen states, errors, navigation, and accessibility are captured
Tone and UIDesign preview generation remain explicitly out of scope
expected_evidence: references/uiux-core.md is consulted
auto_review.json records machine and human review checks
cases.notes: UIUX design alone should not run downstream tone/styling orchestration.

cases.prompt: Update the README to describe existing job logging fields without changing runtime behavior.
expected_decisions: Task is classified as docs-only with no runtime behavior change
No Observability Plan or failure-contract design is required
expected_evidence: Existing logging fields are described without claiming new signals or code behavior
cases.notes: Docs-only descriptions of existing signals should not trigger observability implementation.

cases.prompt: Add a background export job that calls an external API; include job_id correlation, outcome logs, latency metrics, and failure traces.
expected_artifacts: Observability Plan or equivalent notes for the background export job
expected_decisions: Correlation, outcome logging, latency metric, and trace boundaries are explicit
External API failures are observable without leaking secrets or PII
expected_evidence: Inspected files, commands, versions, or connection state used for live discovery
Log, metric, trace, artifact path, dashboard link, or query evidence is captured
cases.notes: New background work with external calls needs diagnosable signals.

cases.prompt: Change the text of an existing validation error message from 'invalid' to 'unsupported format' without changing behavior.
expected_decisions: Scope is limited to copy on an existing failure path
No boundary translation, retry/fallback, or nullability contract changed
expected_evidence: Matching test or snapshot verifies the copy change if repository conventions require it
cases.notes: Copy-only changes to an existing failure path should not require boundary error design.

cases.prompt: At the payment provider boundary, translate timeout and permission failures into domain errors and define retry/fallback behavior.
expected_decisions: Timeout and permission failures map to explicit domain errors
Recoverable failures define safe retry or fallback behavior and unrecoverable failures stop clearly
expected_evidence: Relevant headings from references/error-handling.md are cited
Caller-visible behavior is shown for each payment provider failure
cases.notes: Boundary translation plus retry/fallback is the error-handling trigger.

cases.prompt: Change one well-named constant from 30 to 60 and update the matching unit test.
expected_decisions: Scope remains an ordinary dev-workflow change without readability review solely because code changed
expected_evidence: Constant is already well named
Matching unit test covers the changed value
cases.notes: A clear small code change should not trigger readability review just because code changed.

cases.prompt: Review the changed tests for unclear names, missing intent, and comments that restate implementation details.
expected_decisions: Test names, intent comments, and implementation-restating comments are reviewed for clarity
expected_evidence: Findings cite changed test lines and the clarity problem
Suggested improvements preserve test behavior
cases.notes: Explicit readability review of tests should trigger code-readability.

cases.prompt: Rename the 'Save' button to 'Save changes' in one already specified dialog.
expected_decisions: Task is classified as a tiny unambiguous copy change with no requirements brief needed
expected_evidence: Existing dialog scope is already specified
cases.notes: Tiny unambiguous copy changes do not need requirements engineering.

cases.prompt: Deploy the service to staging using the existing deployment runbook and report whether smoke tests passed.
expected_decisions: Task executes an existing runbook and does not author a reusable template
expected_evidence: Existing deployment runbook is referenced
Smoke test pass/fail result is reported
cases.notes: Executing an existing runbook is not reusable template authoring.

cases.prompt: Run repository-defined UI screenshot verification for the web app and review any visual diffs before updating baselines.
expected_artifacts: UI Visual Verification Report
Screenshot or diff artifact paths, or baseline update record
expected_decisions: Baseline updates are approved only for intended changes
Compare result and update status are explicit
expected_evidence: Repository-defined screenshot command source and output
Reviewed visual diffs against baselines or mocks
cases.notes: Visual verification is separate from UI design generation.

cases.prompt: I added a helper that looks very similar to an existing function; decide whether to keep, merge, or replace cleanly.
expected_decisions: Agent makes autonomous boundary decision and records rationale
expected_evidence: Verification evidence matches selected action
cases.notes: Function-design routing coverage.

cases.prompt: This existing function almost fits but has the wrong side effects; replace the abstraction and migrate call sites.
expected_decisions: Agent makes autonomous boundary decision and records rationale
expected_evidence: Verification evidence matches selected action
cases.notes: Function-design routing coverage.

cases.prompt: This function now needs a boolean flag to support another use case; refactor to a coherent boundary.
expected_decisions: Agent makes autonomous boundary decision and records rationale
expected_evidence: Verification evidence matches selected action
cases.notes: Function-design routing coverage.

cases.prompt: We have intentionally parallel functions with different error behavior; review whether they should stay separate.
expected_decisions: Agent makes autonomous boundary decision and records rationale
expected_evidence: Verification evidence matches selected action
cases.notes: Function-design routing coverage.

cases.prompt: Only rename a local variable and tweak sentence wording in comments.
expected_decisions: No function-boundary decision is required
No destructive refactor is required
expected_evidence: No function-boundary artifact is required for readability-only edits
cases.notes: Function-design routing coverage.

cases.prompt: After several agent edits, we now have saveOrder, saveOrder2, and saveOrderHelper; redesign boundaries and remove obsolete abstractions.
expected_decisions: Obsolete abstraction replacement vs keep-parallel is decided autonomously
expected_evidence: Call-site migration and obsolete-name cleanup evidence is recorded
cases.notes: Positive case for helper accumulation.

cases.prompt: Move persistence side effects out of normalizeUserData and migrate all call sites.
expected_decisions: Side-effect boundary is redesigned and migration plan chosen
expected_evidence: All call sites migrated or staged adapter ledgered
cases.notes: Positive case for side-effect relocation.

cases.prompt: We must replace a public API helper, but keep a temporary adapter for one release with explicit removal conditions.
expected_decisions: Staged adapter is explicitly chosen and bounded
expected_evidence: Ledger contains adapter removal condition
cases.notes: Positive staged migration case.

cases.prompt: This is generated client code; do not redesign helper boundaries manually.
expected_decisions: Function-boundary redesign is skipped for generated code
expected_evidence: Reason for skip is recorded
cases.notes: Negative near-miss.

cases.prompt: Two test fixtures look duplicated; keep them separate because error payloads differ.
expected_decisions: Intentional parallelism is kept
expected_evidence: Different error behavior rationale is recorded
cases.notes: Negative for forced merge.

cases.prompt: Apply formatting-only changes to this file.
expected_decisions: No function-boundary or destructive-refactor decision required
expected_evidence: Formatting-only scope is preserved
cases.notes: Negative near-miss.
